title: Register Entity (LLC/Corp)
description: Register your business entity with your state.
whyItMatters: Establishes a distinct legal entity separate from yourself.
suggestedNextStep: Visit your Secretary of State website to file articles of organization.

title: Obtain EIN
description: Get an Employer Identification Number from the IRS.
whyItMatters: Required to open a business bank account and build business credit.
suggestedNextStep: Apply for an EIN for free on the IRS website.

title: Open Business Bank Account
description: Open a dedicated checking account for your business.
whyItMatters: Separates business and personal finances; required by many vendors and lenders.
suggestedNextStep: Gather your EIN and articles of organization and visit a bank.

title: Get a Professional Business Address
description: Set up a physical or virtual business address (not a PO Box).
whyItMatters: Lenders look for a credible business location.
suggestedNextStep: Research virtual office spaces or use your commercial lease address.

title: Set Up a Business Phone Number
description: Get a dedicated business phone number (toll-free or local) and list it in the 411 directory.
whyItMatters: Helps verify your business existence during credit checks.
suggestedNextStep: Sign up for a VoIP service and register with 411.

title: Create a Business Website & Domain Email
description: Register a domain name, create a simple website, and set up an email address like name@yourcompany.com.
whyItMatters: Shows lenders and vendors you are a legitimate, established business.
suggestedNextStep: Buy a domain name and set up a basic landing page.

title: Obtain DUNS Number
description: Register for a free Data Universal Numbering System (DUNS) number from Dun & Bradstreet.
whyItMatters: It's the primary identifier for your business credit profile with D&B.
suggestedNextStep: Apply for a DUNS number on the Dun & Bradstreet website.

title: Apply for Business Licenses
description: Acquire necessary local, state, or federal licenses for your industry.
whyItMatters: Ensures you operate legally and can be verified by underwriters.
suggestedNextStep: Check with your local city hall or county clerk.

title: Apply for Tier 1 Vendors
description: Start building your credit file by applying for introductory net-30 accounts.
whyItMatters: Establishes your initial payment history with credit bureaus.
suggestedNextStep: Review the Vendor Tracker to find starter net-30 accounts.
